volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Hecuba (Section 1282 - Section 1295)

sections.label: Section 1282
sections.original: οὐχ ἕλξετʼ αὐτόν , δμῶες , ἐκποδὼν βίᾳ ;
sections.translation: Ho ! servants , drag away him from my sight !

sections.label: Section 1284
sections.translation: Close it now ; for I have spoken .

sections.label: Section 1285
sections.translation: and cast him upon some desert island , since his mouth is full of such exceeding presumption . Go , unhappy Hecuba , and bury your two corpses ; and you , Trojan women , must draw near your masters ’ tents , for lo ! I perceive a breeze

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.original: πρὸς οἶκον ἤδη τάσδε πομπίμους ὁρῶ .
sections.translation: just rising to waft us home . May we reach our country well and find all well at home , released from troubles here ! Polymestor is dragged away by Agamemnon ’ s guards .

sections.label: Section 1293
sections.original: ἴτε πρὸς λιμένας σκηνάς τε , φίλαι ,
sections.translation: Away to the harbour and the tents , my friends , to prove the toils of

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.translation: slavery ! for such is fate ’ s relentless hest .
